posts.text:
Keeping kids safe online is a worthy goal—but good intentions are not enough to make good policy. Recent bills around the KIDS Act and the App Store Accountability Act fail to keep kids safe and threaten online speech for everyone, Cato scholars explain. 
  
ow.ly/A04R50YwxMo

No tax on tips and overtime may sound great, but they add complexity and unfairness to the tax code. We need a simple, fair, and flat tax that would treat everyone the same, says Cato’s @adamnmichel.

Video

Cato’s new media fellow, @RIKKISCHLOTT, joins @MrRBourne to talk Gen Z: how social media shaped them, why online life has made young people both more anxious and more persuadable, and how the socialist left and the alt-right have each found fertile ground. Listen to the Cato Podcast on Apple, Spotify, and our website. 

Apple: ow.ly/rEBC50Ywuug  
Spotify: ow.ly/EH4S50Ywuue  
Our website: ow.ly/Jsm250Ywuuc

SCOTUS has a key opportunity to hear a First Amendment case that could expand access to justice. 

At stake is whether Americans can be punished for offering basic legal advice. Cato’s amicus brief argues the answer is no: the First Amendment protects such speech, even when it involves professional knowledge. 

 ow.ly/ZXPu50Ywot1

The United States is the largest importer in the world. However, according to 2000-2024 data, the US share of global goods import flows has declined. 

The US market is important to other countries, but in most instances, there are other markets where those countries can sell their goods, writes James Bacchus. 

ow.ly/uCgH50Yt1yN

For too long, excessive regulations have forced banks to hold exorbitant amounts of capital as reserves. @federalreserve Vice Chair for Supervision Michelle Bowman announced the Fed’s upcoming proposal to right-size capital requirements at a @CatoCMFA event last week.

Video

The Cato Institute is pleased to announce that author and commentator @RIKKISCHLOTT is joining the Institute as a Cato Media Fellow. 

ow.ly/M7uZ50Ywi7c

The larger lesson of the Jones Act waiver is straightforward. It will provide real, if temporary, economic benefits. But it also amounts to an implicit acknowledgment that the Jones Act itself is a supply chain impediment, writes Cato’s @cpgrabow. 

ow.ly/TAff50Yw66T

Federal agencies are buying massive amounts of Americans’ personal data from private brokers—sidestepping warrants and Fourth Amendment protections. 

“Our privacy and constitutional rights should not be up for sale — at any price,” writes Cato’s @PGEddington. 

ow.ly/WRi850Yw4Mk

March 18 marks National Public Defense Day. “Power, left unchecked, will always expand until it finds a limit. Today, and every day, public defenders are that limit,” writes the Cato Institute’s @foxmike90. 

ow.ly/Swwc50Yw4b1

President Trump’s new housing executive order telling states to get out of the way is genuinely encouraging.

The best way to improve affordability is to remove government-imposed barriers and cost pressures on new construction. Learn more from Cato's @MrRBourne and @sslivinski:
ow.ly/lXxB50YvRAS

Want to learn more about how the Jones Act drives up prices for Americans? Check out “The Case Against the Jones Act” by the Cato Institute’s @cpgrabow—available on Amazon, Apple Books, and Barnes & Noble. 

Our website: ow.ly/QZuI50Yw2nB
Amazon: ow.ly/icqB50Yw2nA  
Apple Books: ow.ly/gEES50Yw2nG  
Barnes & Noble: ow.ly/T6El50Yw2ny

The Trump administration’s decision to waive the Jones Act for the transportation of fertilizer and certain energy products is an implicit admission that the law is a glaring failure, says Cato’s @cpgrabow.

“Congress must require DHS to focus on real threats, and it must empower Americans to sue federal agents for violating the Constitution. We need accountability and constitutionality to restore trust,” testified Cato’s @David_J_Bier before the Senate Budget Committee. 

Read full testimony: 
ow.ly/Qe0r50YtzV1

A federal judge ordered the Texas Comptroller's Office to extend the family application deadline for the Texas Education Freedom Accounts Program to March 31 and to allow Islamic schools to apply to participate. This is good news for Texans, liberty, and equality under the law, says Cato’s @NealMcCluskey. 
ow.ly/KoPV50YvQR6

The annual chance of being murdered in a foreign-born terrorist attack during the second Trump administration is about one in 85 million per year, according to Cato’s @AlexNowrasteh.  

ow.ly/7aZA50YuTho

The Cato Institute welcomed Federal Reserve Board Vice Chair for Supervision Michelle W. Bowman for a conversation on her comprehensive review of the bank capital framework and Basel III.

She shared that “These changes to the capital framework eliminate overlapping requirements, right-size calibrations to match actual risk, and comprehensively address long-standing gaps in our prudential framework. The result is more efficient regulation and banks that are better positioned to support economic

Kevin Warsh’s nomination hearing for Fed chair is the moment for lawmakers to press on key issues. Cato's @JaiKedia offers questions that would make the hearing truly useful: 
ow.ly/bTLG50Yvub6

Consumer Technology Association @CTATech CEO @GaryShapiro emphasized the importance of Section 230 law for smaller tech companies at the Cato Institute event. 

Watch the discussion on our YouTube: piped.video/live/ZB1-S8UWtMo

Video
